"1.0" has a trailing zero after decimal, which is not allowed because all digits are significant and no trailing zeros. So "10" should become "1e1" (integer base) or "1.0e1" is invalid because of trailing zero. So the code incorrectly outputs "1.0e0" which is wrong exponent and wrong base. reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: This extra increment makes 'end' one greater than the actual string length, causing out-of-bounds access in later loops that use 'end'.]
